fullDescription.text: Identifies patterns and behaviors indicative of malware, including unauthorized system access, data manipulation, or resource abuse.

shortDescription.text: Detects attempts to exfiltrate sensitive data.

fullDescription.text: Identifies code attempting to send sensitive information to external, potentially unauthorized, destinations.

shortDescription.text: Detects vulnerabilities or capabilities for remote code execution.

fullDescription.text: Identifies code that could allow an attacker to execute arbitrary commands on the host system remotely.

shortDescription.text: Detects mechanisms for maintaining unauthorized access.

fullDescription.text: Identifies code designed to establish or maintain long-term presence on a system, such as modifying startup entries or creating scheduled tasks.

shortDescription.text: Detects insecure coding practices that could lead to vulnerabilities.

fullDescription.text: Identifies practices such as hardcoded credentials, insecure secret storage, or bypassing secure authentication mechanisms, which can introduce security risks.

shortDescription.text: Detects potential supply chain integrity issues.

fullDescription.text: Identifies discrepancies in metadata, conflicting source repositories, or inconsistent file paths that could indicate repackaging, misconfiguration, or an attempt to obscure the true origin of a skill. Such ambiguities can lead to users inadvertently installing a modified or untrusted version of the skill, posing a supply chain risk.
